metric_name: Phase 1: Intel-as-Standardizer (1981-2005)
metric_value: x86 + open OS + volume economics displace proprietary systems; thesis fully validated by 2005
notes: Maps to ARG-1 (economic winner) and ARG-2 (proprietary platforms fund displacement) in core arguments framework

metric_name: Phase 2: Intel-as-Incumbent (2006-2016)
metric_value: Tick-tock delivers predictable gains; mobile attempted and fails; Itanium drags; Atom doesn't scale; smartphone exit
notes: Mid-phase between economic-winner ascension and challenger-phase displacement

metric_name: Phase 3: Intel-as-Challenger (2017-2026)
metric_value: Tick-tock abandoned 2016; smartphone exit 2019; Itanium discontinued 2021; foundry pivot 2021; AI compute open
notes: Intel itself becoming the proprietary incumbent against more-economic alternatives (ARM, NVIDIA, TSMC)

metric_name: Kastner-vs-Intel prediction asymmetry
metric_value: Intel made dated promises (4 refuted); Kastner made dated predictions (0 refuted) — the entire methodological argument of ARG-10 in one entity
notes: Cleanest test of ARG-10 (analyst credibility earned with named winners, dates, and numbers)

metric_name: Kastner 1981 economic-standardization call
metric_value: Economics of standardized x86 architecture plus open OS would overcome proprietary minicomputers

metric_value: The war that would define the 1990s was x86-versus-everything-else; DEC beat IBM and was then itself beaten by x86
notes: DEC sold to Compaq 1998; Compaq absorbed into HP 2002; VAX/Alpha EOL across 2000s — exact tracking

metric_name: Kastner implicit Itanium counter-position

metric_name: Synthesis — every dated Kastner x86 projection verified or in-flight
metric_value: Intel growth 1990-2010 mapped precisely to Kastner 1981/1988/1993 projections; Intel struggles 2010-2026 map to inverse ARG-1 (Intel itself becoming proprietary incumbent against ARM, NVIDIA, TSMC)
notes: This is the substantive answer to the user question: how Intel growth mapped to Kastner market projections impacting x86 markets

metric_name: Limitation #1
metric_value: Threading uses bag-of-words text matching

metric_name: Limitation #2
metric_value: THREAD-11 (foundry) is undersized — only 4 observations
notes: Mitigation: Legacy archive predates 2021 Gelsinger pivot; flagged as build-out target

metric_name: Limitation #3
metric_value: THREAD-10 (AI compute) is mostly forward-looking
notes: Mitigation: Many high predictions, few verified outcomes — Intel AI story still being written as of May 2026

metric_name: Limitation #4
metric_value: Coverage uneven across decades — 2000s 3x density of 2010s
notes: Mitigation: Reflects peak Kastner Aberdeen output 2000-2007, not Intel's strategic importance

metric_name: Limitation #5
metric_value: Intel-issued claims vs Kastner-issued predictions not formally tagged
notes: Mitigation: Section 6 'zero Kastner refutations' relies on methodology in kastner-prescience-methodology-demo; strict tagging pass would require dedicated audit study
